meta.tagline: Curated knowledge for family, learning, and development

items.title: 3 AI Prompts to Turn Claude Into Your Personal Memory Coach (Using a 2,500-Year-Old Trick)
items.excerpt: Use Claude to build a memory palace. Copy-paste prompts for vivid imagery, spaced repetition, and automated review quizzes.
items.summary: The article provides copy-paste AI prompts that use the ancient memory palace technique to help students build vivid mental associations for memorization. The approach combines classical mnemonic methods with modern AI tools to create personalized spaced repetition and automated review systems.
items.pullQuote: Ancient memory palace technique enhanced with AI-powered spaced repetition

items.title: How America Forgot Its First Black Literary Star
items.excerpt: Is it because her opinions don't fit the narrative?
items.summary: The piece examines how a pioneering Black literary figure has been largely erased from American literary history. The author suggests this erasure may stem from the writer's opinions not aligning with contemporary narratives about Black literature and history.
items.pullQuote: Literary erasure when opinions don't fit contemporary narratives

items.title: The 2026 MLB Rookie Class Could Be One Of The Best Ever
items.excerpt: Loaded with Top 100 Prospects, the 2026 rookie class looks like one of the best this century. The post The 2026 MLB Rookie Class Could Be One Of The Best Ever appeared first on College Baseball, MLB Draft, Prospects - Baseball America.
items.summary: The 2026 MLB rookie class features an unusually high concentration of Top 100 prospects making their debuts. Baseball America analysts project this cohort could rank among the strongest rookie classes of the 21st century based on prospect depth and talent quality.
items.pullQuote: 2026 rookie class looks like one of the best this century

items.title: All 19 Potential PPI-Eligible MLB Rookies Who Made 2026 Opening Day Rosters

items.title: What Is Claude Computer Use? How to Control Your Desktop with AI
items.excerpt: What it is, how to turn it on, what to ask it to do, and how to use it without making a mess
items.summary: Claude's new Computer Use feature allows the AI to directly control desktop applications, browsers, and operating system functions through visual interface recognition. The capability enables automated workflows but requires careful setup to avoid unintended system modifications.
items.pullQuote: AI can now directly control your desktop applications and workflows

items.title: Show HN: Relay – The open-source Claude Cowork for OpenClaw
items.excerpt: Article URL: https://github.com/SeventeenLabs/relay Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47528683 Points: 7 # Comments: 2
items.summary: Relay is an open-source project that provides collaborative AI workspace functionality similar to Claude's enterprise features. The GitHub repository has minimal community engagement with only 7 points and 2 comments on Hacker News.
items.pullQuote: Open-source alternative to Claude's collaborative AI workspace tools

items.summary: Y Combinator-backed recruiting platform Ashby is seeking engineers with product decision-making authority for their technical team. The job posting has received no engagement on Hacker News with zero points and comments.
items.pullQuote: Engineers sought who can make independent product decisions

items.title: Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk
items.excerpt: Article URL: https://www.npr.org/2026/03/25/nx-s1-5752369/ice-surveillance-data-brokers-congress-anthropic Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47527130 Points: 63 # Comments: 28
items.summary: Federal agencies including ICE purchase large datasets about American citizens from commercial data brokers, bypassing traditional warrant requirements. The practice raises significant privacy concerns and has prompted congressional scrutiny of government surveillance methods.
items.pullQuote: Government bypasses warrants by purchasing citizen data from brokers

items.title: How Do We Protect Children from Becoming Addicted to Social Media?
items.excerpt: Liability judgments and warning labels are not enough
items.summary: Robert Reich argues that current approaches like liability judgments and warning labels are insufficient to protect children from social media addiction. The piece examines policy solutions needed to address the growing crisis of youth digital dependency.
items.pullQuote: Liability judgments and warning labels are not enough

items.title: Flying Already Sucks. Trump Just Made it Even Worse.
items.summary: Reich analyzes recent changes to aviation policy under Trump that he argues will further degrade the flying experience for passengers. The piece examines regulatory rollbacks affecting airline service quality and passenger protections.
items.pullQuote: Trump just made flying even worse

items.title: Is Putin Getting Nervous?
items.excerpt: A Putin stooge goes rogue, the war effort flounders, Russians grumble over dead cows and dead phone signals. What’s going on?
items.summary: The analysis examines signs of instability in Putin's regime, including defections by loyalists and growing domestic unrest over infrastructure failures. Russian citizens increasingly express frustration over basic services while the war effort shows signs of strain.
items.pullQuote: Russians grumble over dead cows and dead phone signals

items.title: The Narrative Crisis of Modern Baseball
items.excerpt: In the age of sabermetric optimization, can baseball still tell a human story?
items.summary: The piece explores how sabermetric optimization and analytical approaches have changed baseball's storytelling and fan engagement. It questions whether the sport can maintain its human drama and narrative appeal in an era dominated by statistical analysis.
items.pullQuote: Can baseball still tell a human story in optimization age

items.title: Tim Miller: Florida Just Shocked Everyone
items.excerpt: Tim Miller was on with Katy Tur to take on a surprising shift in Florida politics after Democrats flip a seat in Trump’s own backyard.
items.summary: Democrats flipped a legislative seat in Florida, marking a surprising political shift in what has been considered solidly Trump territory. The electoral upset occurred in Trump's own backyard, signaling potential changes in the state's political landscape.
items.pullQuote: Democrats flip seat in Trump's own backyard
